title: Audio orbit coherence experiment — sine tones above chance, white noise at null; CONSISTENT with QA_CORE_SPEC.v1 orbit structure
_fixture_purpose: PASS fixture: canonical positive result. Empirical observation from qa_audio_orbit_test.py (2026-03-25) showing that dynamical signals (sine waves, AM, chirp, pink noise) exhibit orbit-follow rates above chance after histogram equalization, while white noise sits at the null hypothesis. This is CONSISTENT with the QA_CORE_SPEC.v1 state_space claim that cosmos/satellite/singularity orbit structure reflects genuine temporal correlation structure, not amplitude distribution.

observation.summary: After histogram equalization (rank-quantile map to {0..8}), mod-9 orbit-follow rates: sine_880Hz=0.1715, sine_440Hz=0.1647, am_sine=0.1560, pink_noise=0.1512, chirp=0.1473, harmonic_complex=0.1126 — all above chance (0.111). white_noise=0.1063 — at null. sine_880Hz locks to a single orbit (entropy=0.044, switch_rate=0.008). Gap dynamical vs stochastic: +0.0143.

parent_cert.claim: state_space orbit structure (cosmos/satellite/singularity) partitions state space in a way that reflects genuine dynamical structure, not arbitrary labelling

evidence.description: All 6 dynamical signal types show orbit-follow rates above chance (0.111) after amplitude confound removed via equalization
White noise (IID, no temporal structure) sits at null hypothesis rate
sine_880Hz exhibits near-zero orbit entropy and near-zero orbit switch rate — locks to a single orbit, consistent with periodic orbit prediction
Pink noise (1/f) sits between white noise and pure tones, consistent with partial temporal correlation — QA tracks correlation structure, not signal type per se

open_questions: Is orbit_follow_rate measuring something QA-specific or just lag-1 autocorrelation? Non-QA baseline needed.
Speech vs TTS pilot with equalization and autocorrelation baselines is the natural next experiment.

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
verdict==CONTRADICTS implies nonempty fail_ledger (not applicable here)
